title: rubus thyrsoideus subsp. egregius
intro: Rubus thyrsoideus subsp. egregius, also known as the Eragi black raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus thyrsoideus plant, which is native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Rubus thyrsoideus subsp. egregius include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and fruits can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments in East Asian medicine.
